new_classes.definition_en: Waters and water vapour falling under CN 2201 90 00, including water vapour and natural water that has been filtered, sterilised, purified or softened, as well as ice and snow (HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201, paragraphs A and D). These are waters not classified as mineral waters or aerated waters under subheading 2201 10, containing no added sugar, sweetening matter or flavouring.

new_classes.label_en: Other water quality (not mineral, not aerated)
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by water that is neither natural mineral water nor aerated water; includes ordinary water, filtered water, sterilised water, purified water, softened water, and water vapour, corresponding to HS Explanatory Note paragraphs A and D for heading 2201.

coverage_explanation:
The proposed axioms capture the three main discriminating criteria for CN 2201 90 00: (1) the water is of a type other than natural mineral or aerated water (complement restriction on MineralOrAeratedWaterQuality), (2) it bears an OtherWaterQuality — encompassing ordinary, filtered, sterilised, purified, softened water, and water vapour as per HS Explanatory Note paragraphs A and D, and (3) it has no added sugar, sweetening matter or flavouring (NoAddedSugarOrFlavouringQuality), distinguishing it from heading 2202.

Coverage is not complete (score 0.82) for the following reasons:
- The specific enumeration of sub-types (water vapour, ice, snow, distilled/conductivity water) from HS Explanatory Note paragraphs A and D cannot be fully expressed without introducing further quality subclasses for each sub-type, which would over-specify the ontology beyond what the legal text strictly requires for CN discrimination.
- The 'OtherWaterQuality' class is newly coined and functions as a catch-all residual quality, which is semantically correct but is not formally defined as the complement of MineralOrAeratedWaterQuality in the TBox (that complement is captured only as a restriction on the product class, not in the quality taxonomy). A full closed-world treatment would require additional disjointness axioms in the quality hierarchy.
